executiveSummary: The scanner detected an issue related to Dangerous Port Exposed: 22/SSH. This represents a security risk on the target asset.
riskExplanation: The exposure of this module (ports) allows external clients to gather detail, potentially assisting in targeted reconnaissance. SSH exposed publicly can be brute-forced.
businessImpact: Allowing legacy properties to persist increases vulnerability rates, potentially leading to compliance failures under modern regulations like PCI-DSS.
attackScenarios: An attacker retrieves the disclosures from ports to identify misconfigured interfaces and executes tailored exploit scripts.
nextSteps: Restrict access to port 22 using firewall rules (e
Only allow SSH from trusted IP ranges
